content.text:
| MCP:iJirak | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:iJirak | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Features
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:iJirak | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:iJirak | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:iJirak | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:iJirak | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:iJirak | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:iJirak | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:iJirak | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:iJirak | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:iJirak | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:iJirak | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:iJirak | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:iJirak | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:iJirak | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:iJirak | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:iJirak | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:iJirak | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:iJirak | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Quick Start
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```sh
| MCP:iJirak | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:iJirak | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```sh
| MCP:iJirak | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:iJirak | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:iJirak | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:iJirak | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:iJirak | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:iJirak | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```sh
| MCP:iJirak | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | The script will:
| MCP:iJirak | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:iJirak | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:iJirak | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:iJirak | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:iJirak | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```sh
| MCP:iJirak | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```sh
| MCP:iJirak | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:iJirak | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # Run the application
| MCP:iJirak | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```sh
| MCP:iJirak | docker compose up -d
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Project Structure
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:iJirak | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:iJirak | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:iJirak | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:iJirak | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:iJirak | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:iJirak | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:iJirak | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── config/
| MCP:iJirak | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:iJirak | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:iJirak | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:iJirak | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:iJirak | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:iJirak | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:iJirak | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:iJirak | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:iJirak | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:iJirak | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:iJirak | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## API Usage
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:iJirak | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:iJirak | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:iJirak | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:iJirak | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:iJirak | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:iJirak | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:iJirak | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:iJirak | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:iJirak | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```java
| MCP:iJirak | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:iJirak | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:iJirak | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:iJirak | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iJirak | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iJirak | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iJirak | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Idempotency
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```java
| MCP:iJirak | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:iJirak | @Idempotent
| MCP:iJirak | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Kafka Messaging
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | - Kafka and Zookeeper are included in Docker Compose
| MCP:iJirak | - `POST /kafka/send` sends messages to Kafka (supports optional scheduled delivery)
| MCP:iJirak | - Consumer logs received messages
| MCP:iJirak | - Logging level configurable in `application.properties`
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Environment Configuration
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables and Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```sh
| MCP:iJirak | # Run test environment
| MCP:iJirak | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # Run prod environment
| MCP:iJirak | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Code Coverage
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | JaCoCo reports are generated during `mvn test` and available at:
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | spring-boot-template-api/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:iJirak | spring-boot-template-core/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:iJirak | ```
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## Contributing
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for setup instructions, code style, and PR process.
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ---
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | ## License
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

| MCP:iJirak | #!/usr/bin/env bash
| MCP:iJirak | set -euo pipefail
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # =============================================================================
| MCP:iJirak | # init-project.sh — Initialize a new project from SpringBootTemplate
| MCP:iJirak | # Usage: ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact myapp --name "My App" --author "John Doe"
| MCP:iJirak | # =============================================================================
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Color helpers ──
| MCP:iJirak | RED='\033[0;31m'
| MCP:iJirak | GREEN='\033[0;32m'
| MCP:iJirak | YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
| MCP:iJirak | CYAN='\033[0;36m'
| MCP:iJirak | NC='\033[0m'
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | info()  { printf "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC}  %s\n" "$1"; }
| MCP:iJirak | ok()    { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${NC}    %s\n" "$1"; }
| MCP:iJirak | warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC}  %s\n" "$1"; }
| MCP:iJirak | error() { printf "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} %s\n" "$1" >&2; }
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Detect OS for sed -i compatibility ──
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     SED_I=(sed -i '')
| MCP:iJirak | else
| MCP:iJirak |     SED_I=(sed -i)
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Parse arguments ──
| MCP:iJirak | GROUP_ID=""
| MCP:iJirak | ARTIFACT=""
| MCP:iJirak | NAME=""
| MCP:iJirak | AUTHOR=""
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
| MCP:iJirak |     case $1 in
| MCP:iJirak |         --group-id) GROUP_ID="$2"; shift 2 ;;
| MCP:iJirak |         --artifact) ARTIFACT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
| MCP:iJirak |         --name)     NAME="$2";     shift 2 ;;
| MCP:iJirak |         --author)   AUTHOR="$2";   shift 2 ;;
| MCP:iJirak |         -h|--help)
| MCP:iJirak |             echo "Usage: ./init-project.sh --group-id <groupId> --artifact <artifact> [--name <name>] [--author <author>]"
| MCP:iJirak |             echo ""
| MCP:iJirak |             echo "Options:"
| MCP:iJirak |             echo "  --group-id   Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. com.acme)       [required]"
| MCP:iJirak |             echo "  --artifact   Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. myapp, demo-app)       [required]"
| MCP:iJirak |             echo "  --name       Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact)"
| MCP:iJirak |             echo "  --author     Author name for @author tags (default: keeps 'tzesh')"
| MCP:iJirak |             exit 0
| MCP:iJirak |             ;;
| MCP:iJirak |         *) error "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
| MCP:iJirak |     esac
| MCP:iJirak | done
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Interactive prompts for missing required values ──
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -z "$GROUP_ID" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     read -rp "Enter groupId (e.g. com.acme): " GROUP_ID
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -z "$ARTIFACT" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     read -rp "Enter artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. myapp): " ARTIFACT
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Validate inputs ──
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -z "$GROUP_ID" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     error "group-id is required"; exit 1
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -z "$ARTIFACT" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     error "artifact is required"; exit 1
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | if ! echo "$GROUP_ID" | grep -qE '^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(\.[a-z][a-z0-9]*)+$'; then
| MCP:iJirak |     error "Invalid group-id format: '$GROUP_ID' (expected e.g. com.acme)"; exit 1
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | if ! echo "$ARTIFACT" | grep -qE '^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(-[a-z][a-z0-9]*)*$'; then
| MCP:iJirak |     error "Invalid artifact format: '$ARTIFACT' (expected lowercase kebab-case, e.g. myapp or my-app)"; exit 1
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Idempotency check ──
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ ! -d "spring-boot-template-core" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     error "Directory 'spring-boot-template-core' not found. Project may already be initialized."
| MCP:iJirak |     exit 1
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Derive values ──
| MCP:iJirak | ARTIFACT_NO_HYPHENS="${ARTIFACT//-/}"
| MCP:iJirak | PACKAGE_NAME="${GROUP_ID}.${ARTIFACT_NO_HYPHENS}"
| MCP:iJirak | PACKAGE_PATH="${PACKAGE_NAME//.//}"
| MCP:iJirak | ARTIFACT_LOWER="${ARTIFACT//-/_}"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # PascalCase: split on hyphens, capitalize each word
| MCP:iJirak | ARTIFACT_PASCAL=""
| MCP:iJirak | IFS='-' read -ra PARTS <<< "$ARTIFACT"
| MCP:iJirak | for part in "${PARTS[@]}"; do
| MCP:iJirak |     ARTIFACT_PASCAL+="$(tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' <<< "${part:0:1}")${part:1}"
| MCP:iJirak | done
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | CORE_MODULE="${ARTIFACT}-core"
| MCP:iJirak | API_MODULE="${ARTIFACT}-api"
| MCP:iJirak | DB_NAME_PREFIX="${ARTIFACT_LOWER}"
| MCP:iJirak | CONTAINER_PREFIX="${ARTIFACT_LOWER}"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # Derive human-readable name from artifact if not provided
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -z "$NAME" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     NAME=""
| MCP:iJirak |     IFS='-' read -ra PARTS <<< "$ARTIFACT"
| MCP:iJirak |     for part in "${PARTS[@]}"; do
| MCP:iJirak |         NAME+="$(tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' <<< "${part:0:1}")${part:1} "
| MCP:iJirak |     done
| MCP:iJirak |     NAME="${NAME% }"
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # Kafka group id
| MCP:iJirak | KAFKA_GROUP="${ARTIFACT}-group"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Print summary ──
| MCP:iJirak | echo ""
| MCP:iJirak | info "Project initialization settings:"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Group ID:        $GROUP_ID"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Artifact:        $ARTIFACT"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Name:            $NAME"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Author:          ${AUTHOR:-tzesh (unchanged)}"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Package:         $PACKAGE_NAME"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Package path:    $PACKAGE_PATH"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Core module:     $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  API module:      $API_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Container prefix: $CONTAINER_PREFIX"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  PascalCase:      $ARTIFACT_PASCAL"
| MCP:iJirak | echo ""
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Step 1: Text replacements in file contents (before directory renames) ──
| MCP:iJirak | info "Replacing text in file contents..."
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # Build list of files to process (exclude .git, binaries, target dirs)
| MCP:iJirak | FILES=$(find . -type f \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -path './.git/*' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -path '*/target/*' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -path './init-project.sh' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -path './.idea/*' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.jar' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.class' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.png' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.jpg' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.jpeg' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.gif' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.ico' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.svg' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.woff' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.woff2' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.ttf' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '*.eot' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name '.DS_Store' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name 'mvnw' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name 'mvnw.cmd' \
| MCP:iJirak |     -not -name 'maven-wrapper.jar' \
| MCP:iJirak | )
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # Order matters: longer/more-specific strings first to avoid partial matches
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 1. Java package name (fully qualified)
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com\.tzesh\.springtemplate|${PACKAGE_NAME}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced com.tzesh.springtemplate -> $PACKAGE_NAME"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 2. Java package path (slashes)
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com/tzesh/springtemplate|${PACKAGE_PATH}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced com/tzesh/springtemplate -> $PACKAGE_PATH"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 3. Module names (longer first to avoid partial match)
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-boot-template-core|${CORE_MODULE}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced spring-boot-template-core -> $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-boot-template-api|${API_MODULE}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced spring-boot-template-api -> $API_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 4. Parent artifact name (after module-specific replacements)
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-boot-template|${ARTIFACT}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced spring-boot-template -> $ARTIFACT"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 5. Kafka group id
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-template-group|${KAFKA_GROUP}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced spring-template-group -> $KAFKA_GROUP"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 6. Docker container names (underscored variants)
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring_boot_template|${CONTAINER_PREFIX}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced spring_boot_template -> $CONTAINER_PREFIX"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring_template|${CONTAINER_PREFIX}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced spring_template -> $CONTAINER_PREFIX"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 7. Core pom.xml <name> field (uses spring-template-core without "boot")
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-template-core|${CORE_MODULE}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced spring-template-core -> $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 8. Human-readable names
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|Spring Boot Template|${NAME}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced Spring Boot Template -> $NAME"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|Spring Template|${NAME}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced Spring Template -> $NAME"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 9. PascalCase (JWT issuer, GitHub repo name)
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|SpringBootTemplate|${ARTIFACT_PASCAL}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced SpringBootTemplate -> $ARTIFACT_PASCAL"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|SpringTemplate|${ARTIFACT_PASCAL}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced SpringTemplate -> $ARTIFACT_PASCAL"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 10. groupId in pom.xml and log4j2
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com\.tzesh|${GROUP_ID}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced com.tzesh -> $GROUP_ID"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com/tzesh|${GROUP_ID//./\/}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced com/tzesh -> ${GROUP_ID//./\/}"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 11. GitHub URL references
| MCP:iJirak | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|github\.com/tzesh/SpringBootTemplate|github.com/${ARTIFACT_PASCAL}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Replaced GitHub URL references"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # 12. Author tags (only if --author provided)
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -n "$AUTHOR" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     "${SED_I[@]}" "s|@author tzesh|@author ${AUTHOR}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak |     ok "Replaced @author tzesh -> @author $AUTHOR"
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Step 2: Rename Java package directories ──
| MCP:iJirak | info "Renaming Java package directories..."
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | OLD_PKG_PATH="com/tzesh/springtemplate"
| MCP:iJirak | NEW_PKG_PATH="$PACKAGE_PATH"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | for SOURCE_ROOT in \
| MCP:iJirak |     "spring-boot-template-core/src/main/java" \
| MCP:iJirak |     "spring-boot-template-core/src/test/java" \
| MCP:iJirak |     "spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java" \
| MCP:iJirak |     "spring-boot-template-api/src/test/java"; do
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak |     OLD_DIR="${SOURCE_ROOT}/${OLD_PKG_PATH}"
| MCP:iJirak |     NEW_DIR="${SOURCE_ROOT}/${NEW_PKG_PATH}"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak |     if [[ -d "$OLD_DIR" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |         mkdir -p "$NEW_DIR"
| MCP:iJirak |         # Move all contents (files and dirs) from old to new
| MCP:iJirak |         if ls -A "$OLD_DIR" | head -1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
| MCP:iJirak |             cp -R "$OLD_DIR"/* "$NEW_DIR"/ 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak |             cp -R "$OLD_DIR"/.[!.]* "$NEW_DIR"/ 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak |             rm -rf "$OLD_DIR"
| MCP:iJirak |         fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak |         # Clean up empty parent directories
| MCP:iJirak |         OLD_PARENT="${SOURCE_ROOT}/com/tzesh"
| MCP:iJirak |         rmdir "$OLD_PARENT" 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak |         OLD_GRANDPARENT="${SOURCE_ROOT}/com"
| MCP:iJirak |         # Only remove 'com' if groupId doesn't start with 'com'
| MCP:iJirak |         if [[ "${GROUP_ID}" != com* ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |             rmdir "$OLD_GRANDPARENT" 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:iJirak |         fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak |         ok "Moved $OLD_DIR -> $NEW_DIR"
| MCP:iJirak |     else
| MCP:iJirak |         warn "Directory not found: $OLD_DIR (skipped)"
| MCP:iJirak |     fi
| MCP:iJirak | done
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Step 3: Rename module directories ──
| MCP:iJirak | info "Renaming module directories..."
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -d "spring-boot-template-core" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     mv "spring-boot-template-core" "$CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak |     ok "Renamed spring-boot-template-core -> $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | if [[ -d "spring-boot-template-api" ]]; then
| MCP:iJirak |     mv "spring-boot-template-api" "$API_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak |     ok "Renamed spring-boot-template-api -> $API_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | fi
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Step 4: Clean up ──
| MCP:iJirak | info "Cleaning up..."
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # Remove this script (self-delete)
| MCP:iJirak | SCRIPT_PATH="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)/$(basename "$0")"
| MCP:iJirak | rm -f "$SCRIPT_PATH"
| MCP:iJirak | ok "Removed init-project.sh"
| MCP:iJirak | 
| MCP:iJirak | # ── Done ──
| MCP:iJirak | echo ""
| MCP:iJirak | printf "${GREEN}========================================${NC}\n"
| MCP:iJirak | printf "${GREEN}  Project initialized successfully!${NC}\n"
| MCP:iJirak | printf "${GREEN}========================================${NC}\n"
| MCP:iJirak | echo ""
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Project:   $NAME"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Package:   $PACKAGE_NAME"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  Modules:   $CORE_MODULE, $API_MODULE"
| MCP:iJirak | echo ""
| MCP:iJirak | echo "Next steps:"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  1. Review the changes: git diff"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  2. Build the project:  mvn clean compile"
| MCP:iJirak | echo "  3. Run the tests:      mvn test"
| MCP:iJirak | echo ""
| MCP:iJirak | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: ba74aa8cadc5daf37bbe9c15ddd33b162b9fc681)

data.content:
Based on my analysis of the repository, here's a comprehensive summary of how to initialize a new project from this Spring Boot template and start the required infrastructure locally.

## **Project Overview**

This is a **production-ready Spring Boot 4.0.0 template** with Java 25, featuring:
- Multi-module Maven structure (`core` and `api` modules)
- JWT authentication & authorization
- PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka & Zookeeper integration
- Rate limiting, idempotency, correlation IDs
- Docker Compose for containerized infrastructure

## **How to Initialize a New Project**

### **Option 1: Interactive Mode (Recommended for beginners)**
```bash
# 1. Use this template on GitHub (click "Use this template")
# 2. Clone your new repository
git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
cd <your-repo>

# 3. Run the initialization script interactively
./init-project.sh
# The script will prompt you for required values:
# - groupId (e.g., com.acme)
# - artifact name (e.g., my-app)
```

### **Option 2: Command-line Mode**
```bash
./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
```

**Required Parameters:**
| Option | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root | `com.acme` |
| `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case | `my-app` |

**Optional Parameters:**
| Option | Description | Default |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `--name` | Human-readable project name | Derived from artifact |
| `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags | `tzesh` |

### **What the Initialization Script Does:**
1. **Renames Java packages** from `com.tzesh.springtemplate` to your package
2. **Updates module directories** from `spring-boot-template-*` to `<your-artifact>-*`
3. **Modifies Docker container names**, Kafka group IDs, and all references
4. **Replaces `@author` tags** if `--author` is provided
5. **Self-deletes** after completion

## **How to Start Infrastructure Locally**

### **Prerequisites**
- **Java 25** and **Maven 3.8.7+**
- **Docker** and **Docker Compose**

### **Infrastructure Services Included**
- **PostgreSQL** (database) - port 5432
- **Redis** (caching, rate limiting, idempotency) - port 6379
- **Kafka** (messaging) - port 9092
- **Zookeeper** (for Kafka) - port 2181

### **Starting Options**

#### **Option A: Start Only Infrastructure Services (Recommended for Development)**
```bash
# Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, and Zookeeper
docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka

# Then run the application with Maven
./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
```

#### **Option B: Start Everything with Docker**
```bash
# Start all services including the application
docker compose up -d
```

#### **Option C: Environment-Specific Deployment**
```bash
# For test environment
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d

# For production environment
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
```

### **Verification Steps**
1. **Build the project**: `./mvnw clean install`
2. **Start infrastructure**: `docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka`
3. **Run the application**: `./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run`
4. **Access the API**: Open http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html

### **Default Credentials (Development Environment)**
- **PostgreSQL**: 
  - Database: `spring_template_dev`
  - Username: `development`
  - Password: `T3st!ng`

### **API Endpoints**
- **Swagger UI**: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html
- **Actuator Health**: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/actuator/health
- **Actuator Info**: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/actuator/info
- **Actuator Metrics**: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/actuator/metrics

### **Next Steps After Initialization**
1. Review changes: `git diff`
2. Build the project: `./mvnw clean compile`
3. Run tests: `./mvnw test`
4. Register a user via `POST /auth/register`
5. Login via `POST /auth/login` to get JWT token
6. Use the token in Swagger UI's "Authorize" button

This template provides a complete, production-ready foundation with all the infrastructure you need for a modern Spring Boot application.
